_comment: sluice hardened seccomp profile (opt-in via SLUICE_SECCOMP=hardened; browser/audit variants exist). Allow-by-default minus a denylist that is a strict SUPERSET of the OCI/Docker default deny set, so 'hardened' is always >= the engine default it replaces. Blocks in-container namespace creation (userns/CVE surface), tracing, key material, the mount API, modules, kexec/swap, the non-cap-gated exploitation primitives the default also blocks (userfaultfd = kernel-UAF primitive; personality ADDR_NO_RANDOMIZE = self-disabling ASLR; kcmp; *_handle_at), and the obsolete/cap-gated rest. Sessions already run uid 1000 with zero caps, so this is defence in depth. NOTE: blocking unshare/clone(NEWUSER) breaks browser-engine sandboxes (Chromium/Playwright) - use SLUICE_SECCOMP=browser. Targets amd64/arm64 (clone/personality flags = arg0).

syscalls._comment: namespace creation, tracing, key material, mount API, modules, kexec/swap.
the rest of the OCI default deny set: non-cap-gated exploitation primitives + obsolete/cap-gated calls. Makes the denylist a strict superset of the engine default.
clone3 -> ENOSYS so glibc falls back to clone(), which we then filter for CLONE_NEWUSER. Preserves normal thread/fork creation.
block clone() only when CLONE_NEWUSER (0x10000000 = 268435456) is set; normal threads/forks (no NEWUSER) are allowed.
block personality() only when it sets ADDR_NO_RANDOMIZE (0x0040000 = 262144), i.e. self-disabling ASLR; normal personality queries/uses (UNAME26, READ_IMPLIES_EXEC, 0xffffffff) pass.
